facts.text: Denver was founded in 1858 at the confluence of Cherry Creek and the South Platte River during the Gold Rush era.
Nicknamed the “Mile High City,” Denver sits exactly one mile (5,280 feet) above sea level.
Denver won the 1976 Winter Olympics bid but withdrew in 1972 after Colorado voters rejected public funding for the games.
Qwest Corporation, founded in Denver in 1911 as Mountain States Telephone & Telegraph, is now part of Lumen Technologies.
The University of Denver, the city’s first higher‑learning institution, was founded in 1864 and remains a prominent local university.
